notes: Reduce latency-wait from 10 to 5 — biggest untested lever. Sequential chain overhead = N_steps x latency_wait.
Try latency_wait=3 — even lower than 5. Test if lower latency helps or if noise dominates.
Try latency_wait=15 — higher than default 10. If slower than 10, confirms latency_wait matters. If similar, confirms noise dominates.
Test use_mem_recommendations=true with default lat=10. Tight memory (e.g. calculate_ic_sbs: 59GB -> 1.4GB) may reduce queue wait. Isolating mem_recs effect from latency_wait.
Repeat baseline conditions (lat=10, no mem_recs) to measure run-to-run variability. Helps isolate whether lat5/15 results are noise vs signal.
Combine lat5 + mem_recs. mem=True gave 5.1 min at lat10 — does lat5 give additional speedup or is lat5 just noise?
Best params so far (lat5+mem) but with al=20 instead of al=10. Higher array limit = more concurrent jobs. At tile scale with only 150 jobs, al20 should allow 2-3 full batches per rule.
Push al=30. al20+lat5+mem=3.4 min (beat local 4.1). Does al30 go even lower? At tile scale 150 jobs, al30 means many rules can submit entire batches in 1-2 shots.
Repeat al20+lat5+mem to verify 3.4 min is real and not noise. al30 regressed to 5.8 min (deadlock), so al20 is sweet spot.
Probe boundary between al20 (3.4 min) and al30 (5.8 min). al25 may hit or avoid the deadlock zone.
Add max_status_checks=2 to winning config (al20+lat5+mem). Reduces Slurm API polling. At tile scale may not matter much but worth testing.
Local backend with mem_recommendations. Local baseline was 4.1 min. Does mem_recs change queue time on local (probably not since no Slurm scheduler).
Bracket al ladder: al10=4.6, al20=3.4, al25=3.6. Where does al15 fall? Should be between 4.6 and 3.4.
VERIFY 2.4 min result from al15+lat5+mem. Non-monotonic sweet spot at al=15 (al10=4.6, al15=2.4, al20=3.4) needs confirmation before trusting.
Probe al=12 to complete ladder (al10=4.6, al15=2.4/3.9, al20=3.4). Is there a sweet spot at 12-13?
Test lat=7 midpoint between lat5 (best) and lat10 (default). Confirms whether lat5 is truly better or if the effect is noise at al=20.
3rd repeat of champion config. lat5+al20+mem: [3.4, 3.4] so far. lat7=4.6 shows lat IS real. Need 3rd confirm to rule out the 2 identical results being coincidence.
Try jobs=600 with winning al/lat/mem params. More remote nodes available to snakemake. At tile scale (150 jobs), jobs>=150 is all equivalent, but worth confirming with optimized params.
Try lat=4 — one step below lat=5 winner. lat5 beats lat7, so probing lat=4 to see if lower latency gains more. lat3 failed at al=10, but al=20+mem might be more tolerant.
Try msc=5 — msc=2 was worse (4.0 vs 3.4). What about msc=5? Program.md says default=10/s. Testing 5 as midpoint.
Complete jobs sweep: j200 with winning al20+lat5+mem. Expected same as j400 at tile scale (only 150 jobs exist). Verifies jobs parameter doesn't matter for tile.
Try msc=1 — completing msc sweep (msc=2=4.0, msc=5=3.9, default~3.4-4.6). Very low polling. At tile scale, fewer status checks = less overhead.
VERIFY 2.9 min from msc=1. New record if confirmed. msc=1 reduces Slurm API spam. Need repeat to confirm vs cluster noise.
Fine-grain al between 15 (noisy 2.4-3.9) and 20 (consistent 3.4). al=18 might be more stable than 15 while still exploiting higher concurrency.
4th repeat of champion. Building statistical picture. Previous runs: 3.4, 3.4, 4.6 min. Current cluster load appears to be ~4.6 min tier. Checking if it recovers.
Direct control: al=20+lat5 WITHOUT mem_recs. Compare same-time conditions to al20+lat5+mem (3.9 now). Isolates true mem_recs effect under current cluster load.
Control: al=20+lat=10+mem vs al=20+lat=5+mem. Isolates lat effect at al=20. If ~same as lat5, then lat5 advantage was noise. Expected: lat10 slightly worse.
VERIFY 2.8 min from lat10+al20+mem. Counterintuitive — lat10 beat lat5 at al=20. Need repeat to confirm vs cluster noise.
Overnight repeat of champion (al20+lat5+mem). Building nightly distribution. Cluster load changes through evening.
Continue overnight monitoring. Building distribution: [3.4, 3.4, 4.6, 3.9, 3.9] avg 3.84 min. Cluster consistency check.
Continue: distribution [3.4, 3.4, 4.6, 3.9, 3.9, 2.8]. Just got 2.8 min. Is cluster staying fast or was that brief?
Continue overnight. Distribution [3.4,3.4,4.6,3.9,3.9,2.8,3.6] avg 3.66 min. Checking if evening cluster is faster.
Try jobs=150 — exactly the tile job count. Does tighter job limit improve scheduler efficiency? j200=4.8, j400=3.9, j600=3.9. Maybe j150 is even tighter.
Test lat=8 — between lat5 (avg 3.69) and lat10 (2.8/3.9). Does a lat=8 split the difference? Under current cluster conditions (3.9 min typical).
Overnight loop: champion repeat. lat5=lat8=lat10=3.9 under current conditions. Distribution building.
Overnight loop: 10th run of champion. Distribution [3.4,3.4,4.6,3.9,3.9,2.8,3.6,3.9,3.9] avg 3.70 min. Currently in 3.9 min steady state.
r10=3.6, cluster may be clearing. Continuing overnight. Full dist: [3.4,3.4,4.6,3.9,3.9,2.8,3.6,3.9,3.9,3.6] avg 3.60.
r11=5.1 (5pm cluster peak). Checking if cluster recovers. Continuing overnight loop.
r12=4.0 (recovering from 5pm peak). Overnight loop continues. 13th champion repeat.
Repeat baseline (al10+lat10+nomem) under current 5pm conditions. Comparing to winner (3.9 min now). Confirms improvement is real and not just lower cluster load.

Fresh run of the winning config (al20+lat5+mem) end-to-end on config_tile to verify the full tracking path — clean output dir, unlock, mem_recs applied, trial_dir logs, wall time recorded to results.tsv.
